input: Draft a follow-up email to a potential client, Sarah Chen, who attended a discovery call yesterday about our AI development services. The call went well. We discussed a potential $15k project to build an internal tool. She said she'd loop in her CTO. Keep it brief and professional.
criteria: references the discovery call specifically (not generic follow-up)
mentions the $15k project or internal tool context to show it was personalized
anticipates the CTO loop-in and makes it easy (no added friction)
includes a clear and specific next step or CTA
does not use filler phrases like 'I hope this email finds you well' or 'Per our conversation'
judge_prompt: You are evaluating a business email drafted by an AI writing assistant. Score 0-10. This tests professional email craft. Full marks: all criteria met, email reads naturally, CTA is specific (not 'let me know'). Deduct 1 point per missing criterion. Deduct 2 points for opening with a cliche phrase. Deduct 2 if over 200 words.
